name: Ella Fitzgerald
abstract: Ella Jane Fitzgerald (April 25, 1917 – June 15, 1996) was an American jazz vocalist, songwriter and composer, sometimes referred to as the "First Lady of Song", "Queen of Jazz", and "Lady Ella". She was noted for her purity of tone, impeccable diction, phrasing, timing, intonation, and a "horn-like" improvisational ability, particularly …

about_quotes.speaker: Dianne Reeves
about_quotes.evidence: Because sometimes in the industry, here you have some young singers that are coming up and they say well they’re the next Sarah and the next Ella... Because when you think of people like Ella and Carmen and all of these great — Betty Carter, Abbey Lincoln, all of these great singers who have spent t
about_quotes.doc_title: Dianne Reeves interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Clinton, New York, October 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Wofford
about_quotes.evidence: Actually that was the first time I had worked with Ella. ... I think Norman Granz knew he was going to have to find someone for Ella because Paul Smith was leaving, the accompanist who had been with her. And I think he hired me to do the CD to see how we worked together.
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Wofford interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 13, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Harry "Sweets" Edison
about_quotes.evidence: I recorded a lot with Nat King Cole, Ella Fitzgerald... I’ve recorded many, many, many times with Ella. In fact I made her last CD, which is called “All That Jazz.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Harry "Sweets" Edison interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 3, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Brown
about_quotes.evidence: we would start the second half with Ella Fitzgerald... they’d say and Ella Fitzgerald. And she would come out and we would play as she was coming out.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Brown interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, AZ, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Williams
about_quotes.evidence: I sang with Carmen, with Sarah and Ella... 'Party Blues' with Ella, and 'Too Close for Comfort.' Stuff we did for the 1957 Metronome All-Stars.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Williams interviewed by Leslie Gourse, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Rebecca Kilgore
about_quotes.evidence: my highest ideal is Ella Fitzgerald... I think I discovered Billie Holiday and Ella Fitzgerald and Frank Sinatra in high school somehow.
about_quotes.doc_title: Rebecca Kilgore interviewed by Monk Rowe, Chautauqua, New York, September 13, 1997
